title: Choosing time over money in a city that rewards money
summary: Reflect on a deliberate decision to trade a higher salary for more time in a city where most signals tell you to do the opposite.
prompt: Write 3 paragraphs (350–500 words) on a deliberate decision to trade a higher salary for more time, what made it difficult, and what the trade actually felt like a year later. Use the past perfect, the third conditional, the present perfect, and 'however' or 'although'.
hintStarters: Two and a half years ago I turned down a promotion in Buenos Aires that would have raised my salary by almost thirty percent, and I am still asked, occasionally, why I…
If you had told me at twenty-eight that I would one day choose three extra hours a week with my parents over a meaningful raise, I would have…
Although giving up the money was harder than I expected, what I have come to understand is that the trade was not really between salary and time but between…

hintVocab.meaningVi: từ chối một sự thăng chức
cân bằng công việc và cuộc sống
sống dưới mức thu nhập của mình
có mặt thực sự, hiện diện
nghi ngờ một quyết định đã đưa ra
đặt câu hỏi về ưu tiên của mình
một sự giàu có thầm lặng

sampleText:
Two and a half years ago I turned down a promotion in Buenos Aires that would have raised my salary by almost thirty percent, and I am still asked, occasionally, why I did it. The new role came with longer hours, two additional reports, and an expectation that I would be available on Sundays for international calls. I had been working at the company for six years, and I had already noticed that the people one level above me, the level I was being offered, were not visibly happier or wealthier in any way that I envied. They had more salary and less time, and they spoke about the trade-off with a kind of tired pride. When I declined the promotion, my manager assumed I was negotiating. I was not. I simply did not want the role.

If you had told me at twenty-eight that I would one day choose three extra hours a week with my parents over a meaningful raise, I would have laughed at you and assumed I was being lectured. At that age I would have called the choice a sign of low ambition. What I had not yet understood was that money and time do not convert into each other smoothly. An extra twenty percent of salary, in my city, mostly disappeared into incremental upgrades — a slightly nicer flat, faster taxis, more restaurants — that did not change my life in any measurable way. The three hours, by contrast, were the hours I needed to call my mother properly on a Tuesday evening instead of replying to her message at midnight with an apology. Although the salary trade looked enormous on paper, the experience of living through it was much smaller than the paper made it appear.

What I have learned from two years on the other side of that choice is more boring than I would have predicted. I am not visibly more relaxed; I still work hard. However, I am present in a way I was not before, and the people who depend on me — at home and at work — have noticed it without being able to name what changed. There is a counter-objection worth acknowledging. People with serious financial obligations, or who live in cities where the cost of living climbs every year, do not have the freedom I had to refuse the promotion. The choice between money and time is, for many people, not a real choice at all. What I would say is that for those who do have the choice, the cultural pressure to take the money is so strong that even noticing the alternative requires effort. Choosing time, when you can, is unusual not because it is wrong but because almost nothing in the city is set up to suggest it.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: an expectation that I would be available on Sundays for international calls

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Third conditional for an imagined past reaction
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'If you had told me at twenty-eight that I would one day choose three extra hours a week, I would have laughed at you' là điều kiện loại 3 — 'had + quá khứ phân từ' ở mệnh đề if và 'would have + quá khứ phân từ' ở mệnh đề chính — diễn đạt một phản ứng tưởng tượng không xảy ra.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'If you had told me at twenty-eight that I would one day choose three extra hours a week, I would have laughed at you' is the third conditional — 'had + past participle' in the if-clause and 'would have + past participle' in the main — to express an imagined reaction that did not happen.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past perfect continuous and past perfect for context
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I had been working at the company for six years, and I had already noticed that…' kết hợp quá khứ hoàn thành tiếp diễn ('had been working') với quá khứ hoàn thành ('had noticed') để tạo bối cảnh trước thời điểm chính được kể trong văn bản.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I had been working at the company for six years, and I had already noticed that…' combines past perfect continuous ('had been working') with past perfect ('had noticed') to establish context before the main narrative moment.

sampleTranslationVi:
Hai năm rưỡi trước, tôi đã từ chối một sự thăng chức ở Buenos Aires đã sẽ tăng lương tôi gần ba mươi phần trăm, và tôi vẫn thỉnh thoảng được hỏi, vì sao tôi đã làm vậy. Vai trò mới đi kèm với giờ làm dài hơn, hai báo cáo bổ sung, và một kỳ vọng rằng tôi sẽ có mặt vào Chủ nhật cho các cuộc gọi quốc tế. Tôi đã làm việc tại công ty được sáu năm, và tôi đã nhận ra rằng những người ở cấp trên tôi một bậc, cấp tôi đang được đề nghị, không thấy rõ hạnh phúc hơn hay giàu hơn theo bất kỳ cách nào tôi ghen tị. Họ có nhiều lương hơn và ít thời gian hơn, và họ nói về sự đánh đổi với một loại tự hào mệt mỏi. Khi tôi từ chối sự thăng chức, quản lý của tôi cho rằng tôi đang thương lượng. Tôi không phải. Tôi chỉ đơn giản là không muốn vai trò đó.

Nếu bạn đã nói với tôi ở tuổi hai mươi tám rằng một ngày nào đó tôi sẽ chọn ba giờ thêm mỗi tuần với bố mẹ thay vì một sự tăng lương đáng kể, tôi đã sẽ cười bạn và cho rằng tôi đang bị giảng giải. Ở tuổi đó tôi đã sẽ gọi lựa chọn đó là dấu hiệu của ít tham vọng. Điều tôi chưa hiểu là tiền và thời gian không chuyển đổi qua lại một cách trơn tru. Hai mươi phần trăm lương thêm, ở thành phố tôi, phần lớn biến mất vào những nâng cấp nhỏ — một căn hộ đẹp hơn chút, taxi nhanh hơn, nhiều nhà hàng hơn — không thay đổi cuộc sống tôi theo bất kỳ cách nào có thể đo lường. Ngược lại, ba giờ đó là những giờ tôi cần để gọi cho mẹ tử tế vào tối thứ Ba thay vì trả lời tin nhắn của bà vào nửa đêm bằng một lời xin lỗi. Mặc dù sự đánh đổi lương trông khổng lồ trên giấy, trải nghiệm sống qua nó nhỏ hơn nhiều so với những gì trên giấy tờ.

Điều tôi đã học được từ hai năm ở phía bên kia của lựa chọn đó nhàm chán hơn tôi đã dự đoán. Tôi không có vẻ thư giãn hơn rõ rệt; tôi vẫn làm việc chăm chỉ. Tuy nhiên, tôi có mặt theo một cách tôi không có trước đây, và những người phụ thuộc vào tôi — ở nhà và ở chỗ làm — đã nhận ra điều đó mà không thể gọi tên được điều gì đã thay đổi. Có một phản đối đáng ghi nhận. Những người có nghĩa vụ tài chính nghiêm trọng, hoặc sống ở các thành phố nơi chi phí sinh hoạt tăng mỗi năm, không có sự tự do tôi đã có để từ chối sự thăng chức. Lựa chọn giữa tiền và thời gian, với nhiều người, không phải là một lựa chọn thực sự. Điều tôi sẽ nói là với những người có lựa chọn, áp lực văn hóa nhận tiền mạnh đến nỗi ngay cả việc nhận ra lựa chọn thay thế cũng cần nỗ lực. Chọn thời gian, khi bạn có thể, là bất thường không phải vì nó sai mà vì gần như không có gì trong thành phố được sắp đặt để gợi ý điều đó.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses the third conditional correctly?
options: If you had told me at twenty-eight, I would have laughed at you.
If you would have told me at twenty-eight, I would have laughed at you.
If you had told me at twenty-eight, I will have laughed at you.
If you had told me at twenty-eight, I would laughed at you.
mcQuestions.explanation: Third conditional: 'had + past participle' in the if-clause; 'would have + past participle' in the main.
mcQuestions.hint: 'If + had + past participle, would have + past participle'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses past perfect continuous correctly?
options: I had been working at the company for six years.
I have been working at the company for six years at that point.
I had been work at the company for six years.
I had being working at the company for six years.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'would have + past participle' correctly?
options: A promotion that would have raised my salary by almost thirty percent.
A promotion that would has raised my salary by almost thirty percent.
A promotion that would have rise my salary by almost thirty percent.
A promotion that would had raised my salary by almost thirty percent.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Would have + past participle' is the structure for unreal past situations; the past participle of 'raise' is 'raised'.

options: Although the salary trade looked enormous on paper, the experience of living through it was much smaller.
Although the salary trade looked enormous on paper, but the experience of living through it was much smaller.
Although, the salary trade looked enormous on paper the experience of living through it was much smaller.
The salary trade looked enormous on paper although however the experience of living through it was much smaller.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Although + clause, main clause' — no 'but', no comma after 'although', no doubling.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'however' correctly to introduce a contrast?
options: However, I am present in a way I was not before.
However I am present in a way I was not before.
However; I am present in a way I was not before.
However, I am presently in a way I was not before.
mcQuestions.explanation: Sentence-initial 'however' takes a comma. 'Present' (adjective) is correct after 'am'; 'presently' is an adverb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses the present perfect correctly for accumulated knowledge?
options: What I have learned from two years on the other side of that choice is more boring than I would have predicted.
What I learned from two years on the other side of that choice is more boring than I would have predicted.
What I have learn from two years on the other side of that choice is more boring than I would have predicted.
What I have been learn from two years on the other side of that choice is more boring than I would have predicted.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present perfect = 'have + past participle' ('learned'). 'Have learn' and 'have been learn' are wrong.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'be set up to + base verb' correctly?
options: Almost nothing in the city is set up to suggest it.
Almost nothing in the city is set up to suggesting it.
Almost nothing in the city is set up for suggest it.
Almost nothing in the city is set up suggesting it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Be set up to + base verb' expresses purpose. 'To suggesting' and 'for suggest' are wrong.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'so + adjective + that' correctly?
options: The cultural pressure to take the money is so strong that even noticing the alternative requires effort.
The cultural pressure to take the money is so strong than even noticing the alternative requires effort.
The cultural pressure to take the money is such strong that even noticing the alternative requires effort.
The cultural pressure to take the money is so much strong that even noticing the alternative requires effort.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'So + adjective + that + result' is the standard pattern. 'Such' goes with nouns; 'than' is for comparisons.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option uses 'do not convert into + noun' correctly?
options: Money and time do not convert into each other smoothly.
Money and time do not convert in each other smoothly.
Money and time does not convert into each other smoothly.
Money and time do not convert at each other smoothly.
mcQuestions.explanation: Compound subject 'money and time' is plural → 'do not'; 'convert into + noun' is the correct collocation.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'a way + relative clause' correctly?
options: I am present in a way I was not before.
I am present in the way which I was not before.
I am present in a way that I was not before in.
I am present in a way what I was not before.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'A way (that/which) I was not before' — the relative pronoun can be omitted as object; no need to repeat 'in' at the end.
mcQuestions.hint: 'A way (that) I was not before' — object relative can be dropped.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'turn down + noun'?
options: Two and a half years ago I turned down a promotion.
Two and a half years ago I turned down to a promotion.
Two and a half years ago I turn down a promotion.
Two and a half years ago I turned out a promotion.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Turn down + noun' = refuse. The past tense is 'turned down'; no preposition follows; 'turn out' has a different meaning.

criticalThinkingQuestion: If choosing time over money is mostly available to people who already have enough money, is it really a 'choice' at all — or a privilege dressed as a virtue? What would have to change for the choice to be genuinely open to everyone?
